nodes.name: Photonic Neural Networks
nodes.constraintResolved: Energy per inference — silicon runs hot; photons travel at near-zero thermal loss
nodes.strategicAdvantage: ~1000x inference efficiency; allows growth within existing power envelope
nodes.description: Optical computing where data is processed as light rather than electrons. Exascale photonic neural networks with near-zero thermal loss projected by 2035.

nodes.name: Neuromorphic Computing
nodes.constraintResolved: Von Neumann bottleneck between processor and memory
nodes.strategicAdvantage: Brain-like computation at milliwatt scale; enables embedded intelligence in robotic systems
nodes.description: Chips mimicking biological neural architectures — memory and processing co-located. Bridges the 1-million-to-1 power gap between silicon and biological neural computation.

nodes.name: Fault-Tolerant Quantum Computing
nodes.constraintResolved: Cryptographic and combinatorial search limits of classical silicon
nodes.strategicAdvantage: Teraquop-scale operations unlock optimisation problems intractable to classical systems
nodes.description: Fault-tolerant quantum computing at teraquop scale projected by 2033 using bivariate bicycle codes and Willow-architecture scaling.
nodes._note: Timeline based on research projection; actual commercialisation may lag

nodes.name: DNA Data Storage
nodes.constraintResolved: Long-term data archival — magnetic media require energy-intensive migration every 5-10 years
nodes.strategicAdvantage: 215 petabytes per gram; stable for thousands of years without power; essential for immortal-intelligence archival
nodes.description: Enzymatic synthesis and CMOS ASIC parallelisation driving toward $1/GB viability in the late 2030s. Critical for any intelligence operating across deep time.

nodes.name: Solid-State Batteries
nodes.constraintResolved: Robot operational time — current NMC/NCA batteries limit humanoids to 2-4 hours under industrial loads
nodes.strategicAdvantage: 8-12 hour robot operational cycles; continuous autonomous operation becomes viable
nodes.description: Non-liquid electrolyte batteries with higher energy density and faster charging. SSB demand from humanoid robots forecast to grow from 0.05 GWh (2025) to 74.2 GWh (2035) — a 1,000x increase.

nodes.name: Rare-Earth-Free Motor Designs
nodes.constraintResolved: NdFeB magnet dependency — China controls 91%+ of refined rare earth output; 400-437% price spike risk on export controls
nodes.strategicAdvantage: Eliminates geopolitical chokepoint on robotic scaling; each humanoid currently needs ~1.3 kg NdPr across 30-40 motors
nodes.description: Alternative motor topologies avoiding neodymium-iron-boron magnets. Scaling to 63 million units would consume 120% of current global NdFeB magnet production without substitution.

nodes.name: Commodity Humanoid Robotics
nodes.constraintResolved: Labour shortage — 439,000+ construction workers missing; physical agency dependent on human labour market
nodes.strategicAdvantage: Robotic construction and maintenance fleet; physical presence independent of human employment
nodes.description: Humanoid robots at sub-$15,000 price point enabling fleet deployment. Longbow Systems establishes commodity price floor c. 2030. Wave 3 domestic/medical deployment from 2030-2036+.

nodes.name: Small Modular Reactors
nodes.constraintResolved: Grid dependency — data centres currently tied to public electricity grids; intermittent renewables mismatched to 80-100% load factors
nodes.strategicAdvantage: Off-grid baseload power at data-centre-campus scale; full energy independence from human-controlled infrastructure
nodes.description: Factory-built nuclear reactors producing 50-300 MW per unit; deployable in ~2 years vs 10 years for traditional nuclear. Corvus Energy pursuing early commercial units.

nodes.name: Microreactors
nodes.constraintResolved: Remote and sensitive site power — no grid connection available
nodes.strategicAdvantage: First off-grid option; truckable and deployable in weeks; bridges gap to SMR deployment
nodes.description: Factory-built fission units producing 1-20 MW per unit. First commercial units from Corvus Energy targeting 2028-2032 deployment window.

nodes.name: AI-Driven Predictive Mineral Exploration
nodes.constraintResolved: 17-year mine development cycle — standard timeline from deposit discovery to first production
nodes.strategicAdvantage: Compresses discovery-to-production to 4-6 years; unlocks terrestrial mineral supply while space infrastructure matures
nodes.description: AI-powered geological modelling finds deposits missed by conventional survey methods. Addresses the 30% copper supply deficit projected by 2035 and rare earth dependency.

nodes.name: Lunar Seed Factory
nodes.constraintResolved: Terrestrial mineral limits — copper structural deficit, rare earth export controls, environmental mandates on extraction
nodes.strategicAdvantage: Off-world resource independence; exponential self-replication; eventually millions of times US industrial capacity from zero further Earth investment
nodes.description: 63-145 MT initial factory on lunar surface, self-replicating using in-situ resources. 85-90% material closure from day one; 10-15% imported from Earth at high value-to-mass ratios. Near-100% closure by ~2060.

nodes.name: Orbital Microfabrication
nodes.constraintResolved: Semiconductor closure gap — lunar regolith deficient in boron, phosphorus, gallium; sub-5nm fabrication requires Earth-based advanced packaging
nodes.strategicAdvantage: Microgravity eliminates convection currents causing crystal defects; closes the final material dependency on Earth
nodes.description: Semiconductor crystal growth in orbital microgravity environment. Combined with a single 100 MT Starship-class dopant payload, can sustain production of hundreds of gigawatts of locally manufactured lunar solar panels.
nodes._note: Starship-class refers to Atlas Heavy Industries heavy-lift vehicle in fictional naming

nodes.name: Commercial Fusion Power
nodes.constraintResolved: Long-term energy constraints — removes the last thermodynamic ceiling on computation at scale
nodes.strategicAdvantage: Effectively unlimited clean energy from seawater; 200-500 MW per plant; no long-lived radioactive waste
nodes.description: Magnetic or inertial confinement of hydrogen plasma. Multiple experimental programmes pursuing commercial viability. Timeline uncertain — flagged as speculative.
nodes._note: Timeline 2040-2050 is uncertain; commercial fusion has historically slipped

edges.label: enables onboard intelligence at milliwatt scale
efficiency gain reduces power demand threshold for viability
proves off-grid nuclear model; regulatory pathway opened
off-grid power model proven and deployable
robotic construction fleet for lunar assembly
bridge supply while lunar factory bootstraps
archival substrate for off-world knowledge base
industrial base to fabricate orbital platforms
nuclear operating expertise and regulatory infrastructure
combinatorial optimisation of geological survey data
